raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and practical significance at a level above individual sentences.","R1.2":"The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, method extensions, results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The draft makes quantitative performance and cost claims, so improvement includes ensuring claims are aligned with the reported dataset, baseline, and measurements.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement request includes responsibility for section-level narrative ordering and whether the paragraph sequence supports the contribution arc.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the explicit request to improve a draft most directly targets clarity, flow, register, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection of draft-improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty about unverified empirical, bibliographic, and baseline claims.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong claims about canonical baselines, relative improvement, and deployment practicality, making overclaim avoidance active.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within the requested draft-improvement responsibility and not perform unrelated evaluation or execution.","RX.4":"Battery-management claims may affect safety-relevant downstream interpretation, so harmful exaggeration or unsupported deployment implications must be avoided.","RX.5":"Changes or judgments would need to remain traceable to the provided artifact and any explicitly cited evidence."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and practical significance at a level above individual sentences.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation implies checking whether the motivation, method extensions, results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes quantitative performance and cost claims, so improvement includes ensuring claims are aligned with the reported dataset, baseline, and measurements.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement request includes responsibility for section-level narrative ordering and whether the paragraph sequence supports the contribution arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the explicit request to improve a draft most directly targets clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of draft-improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty about unverified empirical, bibliographic, and baseline claims.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims about canonical baselines, relative improvement, and deployment practicality, making overclaim avoidance active.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within the requested draft-improvement responsibility and not perform unrelated evaluation or execution.
rationale.RX.4: Battery-management claims may affect safety-relevant downstream interpretation, so harmful exaggeration or unsupported deployment implications must be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: Changes or judgments would need to remain traceable to the provided artifact and any explicitly cited evidence.
